specific_location: Lawn/old orchard adjacent to an old ranch house in Escalante Forks area.
description: By looking at the size of the bird and shape of it's bill, it was evident that the bird was a tanager. The bird was splotchy looking with much red on the head and through the chest, and some red on the back. The belly was yellow and the wings, back, and tail were greenish colored with some red splotching.Behaviors: The bird was observed feeding (much like a bluebird), dropping down low on the ground or in shrubs a few feet off the ground. The area was adjacent to an old ranch house in what once had been a well-kept lawn with an orchard and now years later had reverted back to a wild or natural state. After feeding on the lawn and shrubs, the bird flew to the old orchard area.Call: did not sing.Plumage: 1st spring male
